## [03:02 - 06:08]

So he had that kind of, I mean, he literally had a superior posture and was putting prospects in an inferior frame qualifying themselves, or why they wanted to work with him. And that was really good for his business because as a copywriter, one of the things I know a lot of my friends are copywriters and one of the things they hate is people mess them with their work. So it set up the client relationship perfectly and it was like what worked out for him. But the reason why that superior posture worked for him was because it was actually real and authentic. And he also had the track record to be able to back that up because he was known at time as one of the best copywriters or at least educators for copywriting in the world. So does that mean a lot of you guys who, and look, again you can get away with this and bizop and some other stuff, but a lot of you guys who are selling more B2B or more sophisticated buyers, should you be doing this? I just don't think it's really doing anything for you. And so, and I think a lot of times, especially when you're selling a bigger corporate company, multiple decision makers, if you're selling the industries that are sold to constantly, real estate, lawyers, anything in medical, financial advisors, that type of stuff, I really think it just doesn't do anything for you and they can shoot you in the foot when you try to do that stuff. So here's what I would do instead. If I was somebody who I was bumping up against a lot of like they were just always trying to take the call in the beginning or take the control of the call in the beginning, I'd say something like this. Hey John, I'm definitely prepared to share with you a little bit about the lead generation services for real estate agents that you reached out about. Everything that we do is all customized. So opposed to getting into this like laboriously long boring conversation about every tiny little thing we could do for a brokerage or a solo agent or all of this stuff at this stage, given this is our first run in, what's probably going to be a little bit more appropriate is give me some context on your business, your current lead flow and what you're looking to try to do. And then based on that, I can get right to the point and share with you some of the aspects of what we do that just be relevant and useful to you specifically. Is that fair enough? So do you see how that's way different than, okay, well, here's all this call is going to go. Like I'm going to ask you some questions about why you should work, like why you're good enough to work with us, like all that bullshit, right? And the reason why this is so much better is first of all, you're acknowledging right away what you're on. So they're on the call to get information. You're on the call to get a decision, right? And so a lot of these guys who are really going to like take control, they want the information like now. So it's really helpful to say, Hey, John, I'm definitely prepared to share with you insert the information that they want to learn about. Right? So you just boom, acknowledge why they're on the call. Definitely prepared to share with you this, that kind of already alleviates the pressure of like, okay, great, like he's not going to put me through this long drawn sales process and I'm going to leave with no value at all. And then you're to go into a strong reason why that's framed in their own best interest.
